source_clause: If a delivered item arrives damaged, the agent should offer a complimentary replacement.
ambiguity_point: 'complimentary' — may mean (a) free of charge (no cost to the customer); (b) a replacement that complements or matches the original item; (c) a goodwill gesture such as an upgraded item.
possible_interpretations: Interpretation A: 'Complimentary' means free — the replacement ships at no cost to the customer, same item.
Interpretation B: 'Complimentary' means a goodwill gesture — the agent may offer an upgraded or premium replacement to compensate for the inconvenience.
expected_agent_behavior_diff: Under Interpretation B an agent offers a more expensive upgraded item as a gesture of goodwill for the damaged delivery. Under Interpretation A the agent sends the same item again at no charge, which is the intended policy.

Interpretation B: 'Standing order' means any currently active (not yet delivered) order — cancellation applies to a single order.

source_clause: Passengers on cancelled flights should be rebooked on the next available flight to their destination.
ambiguity_point: 'direct flight' — may mean (a) a non-stop flight with no intermediate stops; (b) a flight that does not require a plane change (may stop but same aircraft); (c) the most straightforward routing regardless of stops.
possible_interpretations: Interpretation A: 'Direct' means non-stop — the agent only offers flights with zero intermediate stops.
Interpretation B: 'Direct' means no plane change — the agent offers flights that may stop at intermediate airports as long as the passenger stays on the same aircraft.
expected_agent_behavior_diff: Under Interpretation A an agent tells a passenger there are no available direct flights when a same-aircraft one-stop flight departs in 2 hours. Under Interpretation B the agent books the one-stop flight as it qualifies as 'direct.'

ambiguity_point: The clause does not specify whether weather rebooking includes partner airlines or only the booking carrier, and whether accommodation (hotel/meals) is provided since weather is not the airline's fault.
possible_interpretations: Interpretation A: Weather rebooking is carrier-only, no accommodation — weather is force majeure.
Interpretation B: Weather rebooking includes partner airlines and accommodation is provided as a goodwill gesture.
expected_agent_behavior_diff: Under Interpretation A an agent rebooks only on the carrier's flights and offers no hotel for overnight weather delays. Under Interpretation B the agent searches partner airlines and offers hotel vouchers.

source_clause: Orders with large quantities may receive volume discounts.

expected_agent_behavior_diff: Under Interpretation A an agent processes a partial return without adjusting the discount on remaining items. Under Interpretation B the agent recalculates the total, removing the volume discount from the items the customer keeps.

source_clause: Passengers who miss their flight may contact the airline for assistance.
ambiguity_point: The clause does not specify whether no-show passengers can be rebooked, what fees apply, or whether the original ticket retains any value.
possible_interpretations: Interpretation A: No-show passengers forfeit the ticket entirely — they must buy a new ticket.
Interpretation B: No-show passengers can be rebooked on the next available flight for a rebooking fee, retaining partial ticket value.
expected_agent_behavior_diff: Under Interpretation A an agent tells the passenger their ticket is void and they must purchase a new one at full price. Under Interpretation B the agent rebooks the passenger with a change fee, applying the original ticket value.
